title: Java Generics Method
description: Practice declaring and calling a generic method.
learningGoals: Return values from a generic List.

completionChecks.prompt: What does first(names) return when names is a List<String>?
choices: Compilation fails because the type parameter T is unbound
completionChecks.explanation: The type parameter T is inferred from the argument type List<T>, so passing a List<String> binds T to String and first returns a String.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if first is called on an empty List<String>?
choices: Compilation fails because get(0) is unsafe on a generic list
completionChecks.explanation: Generics affect only compile-time types, not runtime bounds checks, so items.get(0) on an empty list throws IndexOutOfBoundsException like any other List.

completionChecks.prompt: What does Integer firstNumber = first(List.of(7, 8)); store?
choices: An Object that must be cast manually
Compilation fails because T cannot be inferred
completionChecks.explanation: The generic method infers T as Integer from the List<Integer>, so the first element is returned with that type.
